_about: Curated World Gold Council top-30 central-bank gold holdings (metric tonnes). Bottom-up reference used to drive the gold views for the top holders: WGC supplies clean, current tonnage; IMF IFS supplies the deep history. The loader places each country's `latest_tonnes` at the `_anchor_periods` (gold is sticky, so flat across recent months is accurate for static holders) and overlays `monthly_overrides` for active buyers (China, Poland) where the trajectory matters. Institutions (IMF, ECB, BIS) are intentionally excluded — not central banks / no country code. Taiwan is omitted (not an IMF reporter; would need a fully synthetic series). Limited extracts used under fair-industry-practice with citation.
_vintage: WGC / IMF IFS Dec-2025 edition; China to Apr-2026 (PBoC monthly)
_refresh: Monthly (~2nd week). Update latest_tonnes from gold.org/goldhub Gold-Reserves-by-Country, and the China/Poland monthly_overrides from PBoC/NBP releases. Add an anchor period when the reserves data advances past the last one.

_notes.TUR: WGC flags Turkey: commercial-bank gold deposited at the CBRT inflates the headline; volatile quarter to quarter.
_notes.BRA: Omitted — WGC aggregator figure (~172t) conflicts with IMF (~130t); left to the IMF series to avoid a discontinuity.
_notes.TWN: Omitted — Taiwan (~424t) is not an IMF reporter, so it has no IMF backbone to extend.
